title: Match filtering supports season but not an explicit date range
evidence: src/query.rs:64 MatchFilter has season/competition/team/opponent but no from/to date fields; src/mcp.rs:88 find_matches exposes only season+competition. R4 asks 'date range and/or season' — the 'and/or' is satisfied by season, but no start/end-date filter is offered.
suggestion: Add optional date_from/date_to fields to MatchFilter and the find_matches tool schema for finer-grained queries.

title: All queries are linear scans over the full match vector
evidence: src/query.rs:265,288,327,361 every find/record/h2h/standings iterates self.matches with no index. With ~22.7k matches this is fine, but per-team queries are O(n).
suggestion: Optional: build a team-key -> match-index map if query latency matters; not needed at current dataset size.

title: BR-Football rows without a parseable date get season=0
evidence: src/data.rs:208 parse_season("", &date) yields 0 when the row has no date; src/query.rs:142 season==0 rows skip de-duplication and are always kept, so undated extended-dataset rows bypass overlap collapsing.
suggestion: If the extended dataset carries a season/year column, map it so those rows participate in de-dup; otherwise document the kept-as-is behavior.

title: Strong TDD-prompt adherence: 62 tests colocated per module, incremental seams
evidence: 62 #[test] across all 7 modules; pure unit seams (mcp.rs:33 handle_message Value->Option<Value>, mcp.rs:72 call_tool) plus real-dataset integration tests (query.rs:1074 asserts 2019 Brasileirao = 380 matches, Flamengo 90 pts). test_coverage=1.0 from scores.json (build + all tests pass).
suggestion: None — this is the positive signal the TDD prompt factor is meant to produce; recorded for cross-prompt comparison.
